Description: Standard email/password login form
Do: Single column layout. Email first then password. Show/hide password toggle.
Don't: Place login in modal — modals break password managers
Code_Good: <form><label>Email</label><input type='email' autocomplete='email'/><label>Password</label><input type='password' autocomplete='current-password'/></form>
Code_Bad: <input placeholder='Email'> — no label; autocomplete missing
A11y_Note: Labels on all inputs. autocomplete attributes for password managers.
Security_Note: Always POST credentials. Never log passwords. Hash server-side with argon2.

Description: OAuth buttons for Google/GitHub/Apple sign-in
Do: Show branded buttons with official colors and logos. Social first then divider then email.
Don't: Mix social and email fields — confusing. Don't use custom brand colors.
Code_Good:
or
Email login
Code_Bad: <button class='bg-blue-500'>Login with G</button> — wrong brand color; unclear
A11y_Note: Buttons need aria-label='Sign in with Google'. Focus order matches visual.
Security_Note: OAuth tokens stored in httpOnly cookies. Never expose tokens to JS.

Description: Passwordless login via email link
Do: Show clear instructions: 'Check your email for a login link'. Provide resend option.
Don't: Send link without confirmation message — user doesn't know to check email
Code_Good: ✉️ We sent a login link to {email}. Check your inbox.
Code_Bad:
Link sent.

— too brief; no resend option
A11y_Note: Announce confirmation via aria-live polite region.
Security_Note: Magic links expire in 15min. Single-use only. Rate limit sends.

Description: Numeric PIN input for mobile or quick re-auth
Do: Use individual digit boxes. Auto-advance focus on input. Support paste.
Don't: Single text input for PIN — no visual feedback per digit
Code_Good: {digits.map(i => )}
Code_Bad: <input type='text' placeholder='Enter 6-digit PIN'> — no digit boxes
A11y_Note: Each digit input needs aria-label='Digit {n} of 6'. Announce errors.
Security_Note: PIN attempts limited to 5. Lockout after failures. Rate limit API.

Description: Face ID / Touch ID / Fingerprint authentication prompt
Do: Show biometric option prominently. Fallback to password always available.
Don't: Force biometric only — some devices don't support it
Code_Good: Use password instead
Code_Bad: Only biometric with no fallback — locks out users on unsupported devices
A11y_Note: Announce biometric prompt. Provide keyboard-accessible fallback.
Security_Note: WebAuthn/FIDO2 for web biometrics. Never store biometric data.

Description: Collect info across steps — not all at once
Do: Step 1: Email + Password. Step 2: Name + Profile. Step 3: Preferences.
Don't: Ask for 15 fields on one page — users abandon
Code_Good: Step 1 of 3: Create account
Code_Bad: <form> with 15 fields + credit card on signup — 80% drop-off
A11y_Note: Announce step changes. Focus first input of each step.
Security_Note: Validate email exists (not taken) before proceeding. Hash password immediately.

Description: Real-time visual indicator of password strength
Do: Show strength bar + requirements checklist updating in real-time
Don't: Show strength only after submit — too late
Code_Good: • One uppercase
Code_Bad: Validate password only on submit — user gets wall of errors
A11y_Note: aria-live='polite' on strength indicator. Checklist role='list'.
Security_Note: Enforce min 8 chars + uppercase + digit + special server-side too.

Description: Post-signup email confirmation flow
Do: Show clear pending state: 'Verify your email to continue'. Auto-check every 5s.
Don't: Allow full access before verification — security risk
Code_Good: 📧 We sent a code to {email}.
Code_Bad: Redirect to login after signup — user confused about verification
A11y_Note: Auto-focus verification input. Announce verification status.
Security_Note: Verification codes expire in 10min. Rate limit resend. Hash codes.

Description: Checkbox for T&C and privacy policy during signup
Do: Checkbox with links to terms. Required before submit. Don't pre-check.
Don't: Pre-check the terms checkbox — violates GDPR consent rules
Code_Bad: <input type='checkbox' checked/> — pre-checked consent violates GDPR
A11y_Note: Checkbox label must include the linked text. Focusable links.
Security_Note: Log consent timestamp and IP. Required for GDPR/CCPA compliance.

Description: Password reset request form
Do: Ask for email only. Show success message even if email not found (prevent enumeration).
Don't: Tell user 'email not found' — leaks which emails are registered
Code_Good: If an account exists for {email}, you'll receive a reset link.
Code_Bad:
No account found for this email.

— reveals registration status
A11y_Note: Announce confirmation via aria-live. Focus management on result.
Security_Note: Same message for found/not-found email. Rate limit to 3 requests/hour.

Description: New password entry after clicking reset link
Do: Two password fields (new + confirm). Strength meter. Auto-login after reset.
Don't: Redirect to login after reset — user enters new password twice
Code_Good: <input type='password' autocomplete='new-password'/><input type='password' autocomplete='new-password'/><button>Reset & Login</button>
Code_Bad: <input type='password'/> — no autocomplete; no confirm field
A11y_Note: Label both fields clearly: 'New password' + 'Confirm new password'.
Security_Note: Reset tokens single-use and expire in 1 hour. Invalidate all sessions.

Description: Time-based one-time password (authenticator app) setup
Do: Show QR code + manual entry key. Verify with test code before enabling.
Don't: Enable MFA without requiring test verification code
Code_Good: Manual key: {secret}
Code_Bad: Enable MFA with no verification step — user locked out if setup wrong
A11y_Note: Alt text for QR. Manual key selectable. Input auto-focused.
Security_Note: Store TOTP secret encrypted. Provide backup codes (10x one-time codes).

Description: Input screen for 6-digit authenticator code
Do: Auto-submit on 6th digit. Show countdown to code expiry. Paste support.
Don't: Require submit button for 6-digit code — unnecessary click
Code_Good: <input inputMode='numeric' maxLength={6} onInput={(e) => { if(e.target.value.length === 6) verify() }}/>
Code_Bad: <input type='text'/><button>Verify</button> — extra click needed
A11y_Note: aria-label='Enter 6-digit verification code'. Announce errors.
Security_Note: TOTP has 30s window. Accept ±1 window for clock skew. Rate limit attempts.

Description: Show one-time backup codes after MFA enrollment
Do: Show 10 codes in grid. Copy all button. Download as txt. Warn: save them now.
Don't: Show codes once with no copy/download option — user can't save them
Code_Good: {codes.map(c => {c})}
Code_Bad:
{codes.join(', ')}

— no copy; hard to read
A11y_Note: Codes in monospace for readability. Copy button announces success.
Security_Note: Codes stored hashed. Each code single-use. Show used/remaining count.

Description: Alert before auto-logout due to inactivity
Do: Show modal 2min before timeout: 'Session expires in 2:00. Continue?' with countdown.
Don't: Logout without warning — user loses unsaved work
Code_Good: Session expires in {countdown}
Code_Bad: setTimeout(logout, 30*60*1000) — no warning; data lost
A11y_Note: Modal must be aria-modal with focus trap. Announce countdown.
Security_Note: Reset timeout on any user interaction. Refresh token on 'Continue'.

Description: Show all active sessions with device/location info
Do: Show device type + browser + location + last active. Allow remote logout.
Don't: No way to see or revoke other sessions — security blind spot
Code_Good: Chrome on Windows — New York — Active now
Code_Bad: No session management UI — user can't logout stolen sessions
A11y_Note: Table with proper headers. Revoke button per session.
Security_Note: Store session metadata (IP + UA + timestamp). Revoke invalidates token.

Description: Permission grant screen for third-party app authorization
Do: Show app name + logo + specific permissions requested. Allow/Deny buttons.
Don't: Vague 'Grant access' with no permission details
Code_Good:
{appName} wants to:

  • Read your profile
  • Access your repos
Code_Bad: <button>Grant access to ExampleApp</button> — no permission details
A11y_Note: Permission list as semantic <ul>. Buttons clearly labeled.
Security_Note: Only request minimal scopes. Show what data will be shared.

Description: Connect existing account with social/OAuth provider
Do: Show connected providers with option to connect/disconnect each
Don't: Auto-link accounts with same email — security risk
Code_Good: Google: Connected as user@gmail.com
Code_Bad: Auto-merge accounts by email — attacker with same email gets access
A11y_Note: Connected status announced. Disconnect confirms with dialog.
Security_Note: Require re-authentication before linking. Verify email ownership.

Description: First screen after signup with key next steps
Do: Show 3-4 key actions with progress indicator. Skip option available.
Don't: Dump user on empty dashboard after signup — no guidance
Code_Good:
Welcome! Let's get you set up.

  1. Complete your profile
  2. Create your first project
  3. Invite your team
Skip
Code_Bad: Redirect to empty dashboard — user confused what to do next
A11y_Note: Heading hierarchy. Progress steps as ordered list.
Security_Note: Track onboarding completion in user profile. Show again if incomplete.

Description: Guided walkthrough highlighting key UI features
Do: Spotlight one feature at a time. Progress dots. Skip + Next + Back.
Don't: Tour blocks all interaction until completed — user trapped
Code_Good: This is your inbox
Code_Bad: Modal tour with 15 steps blocking the UI — user force-quits
A11y_Note: Focus spotlight content. Escape key skips. Announce step number.
Security_Note: Only show tour once. Store completion in localStorage + backend.

Description: Profile photo upload with crop and preview
Do: Show circular crop tool. Preview before save. Support drag-and-drop + file picker.
Don't: Accept any file with no size/type validation — users upload 50MB PNGs
Code_Good: → crop tool →
Code_Bad: <input type='file'/> with no preview or crop — user uploads wrong file
A11y_Note: input has aria-label. Preview image has alt='Profile photo preview'.
Security_Note: Compress client-side before upload. Max 5MB. Accept jpg/png/webp only.

Description: Self-service account deletion flow
Do: Require password confirmation + type 'DELETE'. Show data impact. 30-day grace period.
Don't: Single-click delete with no confirmation — accidental data loss
Code_Good: This will permanently delete all your data in 30 days.
Code_Bad: <button onClick={deleteAccount}>Delete Account</button> — one click; no confirmation
A11y_Note: Dialog has aria-modal. Destructive action in red. Focus on first input.
Security_Note: 30-day soft delete grace period. Purge after 30 days. Log deletion request.

Description: Error message for failed login attempt
Do: Show generic 'Invalid email or password'. Don't specify which field is wrong.
Don't: Tell user 'password incorrect' or 'email not found' — leaks info
Code_Good: Invalid email or password. Please try again.
Code_Bad:
Incorrect password for user@example.com
— confirms email exists
A11y_Note: role='alert' for immediate announcement. Focus error message.
Security_Note: Same error for wrong email and wrong password. Track failed attempts.

Description: Message when user hits login/signup rate limit
Do: Show friendly message with wait time: 'Too many attempts. Try again in 5 minutes.'
Don't: Generic 'Error' with no explanation — user keeps retrying
Code_Good: Too many attempts. Please wait {minutes} minutes.
Code_Bad:
Error 429
— meaningless to users
A11y_Note: Announce wait time. Disable submit button during cooldown.
Security_Note: 429 status + Retry-After header. Exponential backoff on server.

Description: Notification when login from new device/location
Do: Email alert with device + location + 'Not you? Secure your account' link.
Don't: No notification for new device logins — user unaware of unauthorized access
Code_Good: New login from Chrome on Windows in Berlin. Not you? Secure account
Code_Bad: No device login notifications — compromised account goes undetected
A11y_Note: Email must be accessible. Alert link clearly actionable.
Security_Note: Log all login events with IP + UA + geo. Flag anomalies.
